title: Cloud AI Services for Working Professionals
modules: The Cloud AI Landscape: How AWS, Azure, and GCP Have Divided the Market
Core Managed AI Services: Vision, Language, Speech, and Prediction APIs
Foundation Model Access and Fine-Tuning on Each Platform
MLOps Pipelines: SageMaker, Azure ML, and Vertex AI Compared
Cost Modeling, Vendor Lock-In, and Multi-Cloud AI Strategy
Security, Compliance, and Data Residency for Enterprise AI Workloads
Selecting the Right Platform for Your Use Case: A Decision Framework
Architecting a Cloud AI Solution for a Professional Scenario
synopsis: AWS, Azure, and GCP each offer mature but divergent AI service ecosystems — and professionals making platform decisions without a comparative framework are routinely locked into suboptimal choices. This course gives technical leads, architects, and decision-makers an honest, side-by-side analysis of capabilities, tradeoffs, and strategic considerations across all three major cloud AI platforms.
rationale: Cloud AI platform selection has multi-year cost and capability implications, yet most professionals default to whichever cloud their organization already uses rather than evaluating fit for AI-specific workloads.
learning_outcome: Learners will be able to evaluate AWS, Azure, and GCP AI services against a structured decision framework and produce a justified platform recommendation for a specific organizational AI workload.
source_signals: Building a Virtual Machine Inside ChatGPT (score: 4803)
nearest_existing: Every Age Believes Its Tool Is the Last Tool
